name: Ginger Salmon Steaks
recipeIngredient: 2 small Salmon Steaks, Each About 3/4-Inch Thick
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the ginger root and green onion into 2-inch long, matchstick-thin strips.
2. Arrange the arugula on a platter.
3. In a 10-inch skillet over medium-high heat, add 1-inch of hot oil. Cook the ginger root and green onion until lightly browned. Remove with a slotted spoon to a small bowl.
4. In the remaining oil, cook the salmon steaks, turning once, until browned and the fish flakes easily when tested with a fork (about 10 minutes). Place on top of the arugula.
5. Discard any remaining oil in the skillet. Stir in the sherry, soy sauce, and 2 tbsp water, blending well and heating through.
6. Pour the sherry mixture over the salmon steaks and sprinkle the ginger root mixture on top.
7. While cooking the salmon, heat salad oil in a 3-quart saucepan. Cook cherry tomatoes until just heated through. Cut hearts out of bread slices and toast.
8. Serve the salmon steaks with toasted bread hearts and cooked cherry tomatoes.
Each Serving Contains:
Calories: About 590
Fat: 26 Grams
Cholesterol: 99 mg. Sodium: 1040 mg.
